env.title: Environment Variables Status
env.subtitle: Check system environment variables configuration
env.tip: Tip: Configure environment variables in Vercel project Settings → Environment Variables. Redeploy after changes.

ui.systemInjectedHint: This variable is automatically set by the runtime / platform; no manual configuration required.
ui.deprecatedHint: This variable is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
ui.renamedHint: The new name is {name}. Migration is recommended.

database.name: Database
database.desc: PostgreSQL connection. At least one of DATABASE_URL or any POSTGRES_* variant must be set.

auth.name: Authentication
auth.desc: JWT signing key and session encryption. Required for the auth subsystem to function.

admin.name: Admin Account
admin.desc: Initial seed admin account created on first database initialization. If unset, the first registered user becomes admin.

app.name: Application URL
app.desc: Canonical site URL. Used for og:url, share links, copyright links, and password reset callbacks.

github.name: GitHub Sync
github.desc: Two-way sync between local content and a GitHub repository. Used for content backup and edits via PR.

giscus.name: Giscus Comments
giscus.desc: Comments widget on post detail pages. All four variables must be set together; otherwise a placeholder is rendered.

storage.name: Storage Pool
storage.desc: Remote storage backend for file assets. Configure via WEBDAV_* variables for WebDAV backends, or set STORAGE_TYPE=b2 with B2_* variables for Backblaze B2. All required variables for the chosen backend must be set together; partial configuration is treated as unconfigured.

smtp.name: SMTP Email
smtp.desc: Outbound email transport for password reset, verification codes, etc. Required only when those features are used.

cron.name: Scheduled Jobs
cron.desc: Shared secret for authenticating calls to /api/cleanup and similar internal endpoints. Unset disables the endpoints.

clerk.name: Clerk Third-Party Login
clerk.desc: Optional Clerk integration for OAuth / social login. Leaving these unset disables Clerk entirely and falls back to local auth.

system.name: System / Build
system.desc: Build- and runtime-injected variables. NODE_ENV / VERCEL_* are auto-set; SKIP_DB_INIT / DISABLE_HMR are developer-only.

database.DATABASE_URL: Primary PostgreSQL connection string used by Prisma at runtime and during migrations.
database.POSTGRES_URL: Vercel Postgres pooled connection (auto-injected by Vercel when Vercel Postgres is provisioned).
database.POSTGRES_PRISMA_URL: Vercel Postgres Prisma-optimized connection URL (auto-injected by Vercel).
database.POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING: Vercel Postgres direct / non-pooled connection URL (auto-injected by Vercel, used for migrations).

auth.AUTH_SECRET: Secret used to sign JWT session tokens. Must be at least 32 characters; rotate to invalidate all existing sessions.

admin.ADMIN_EMAIL: Email address of the initial admin account created during the first database initialization.
admin.ADMIN_PASSWORD: Password of the initial admin account. Must be set together with ADMIN_EMAIL; otherwise the seed step is skipped.

app.APP_URL: Canonical site root URL (e.g. https://example.com). Used for og:url, share links, copyright links, and email callback URLs.
app.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: Legacy name of APP_URL. Still honored for backward compatibility but logs a console.warn on use.

github.GITHUB_REPO: Target repository in the format owner/repo. Required for GitHub sync.
github.GITHUB_TOKEN: Personal Access Token with repo scope. Used to read and write content in the configured repository.
github.NEXT_PUBLIC_GITHUB_REPO: Client-side mirror of GITHUB_REPO for components that render repo metadata in the browser.

giscus.NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_REPO: GitHub repository hosting Discussions, in the format owner/repo. Generate via giscus.app.
giscus.NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_REPO_ID: Repository ID issued by giscus.app. Required together with NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_REPO.
giscus.NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_CATEGORY: Discussion category name (e.g. Announcements). Required together with NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_CATEGORY_ID.
giscus.NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_CATEGORY_ID: Category ID issued by giscus.app. Required together with NEXT_PUBLIC_GISCUS_CATEGORY.

storage.WEBDAV_URL: WebDAV server base URL (e.g. https://dav.example.com/dav). Must point to a directory writable by the configured user. For B2 backend, set STORAGE_TYPE=b2 and configure B2_KEY_ID, B2_APPLICATION_KEY, B2_BUCKET, B2_PREFIX instead.
storage.WEBDAV_USER: Username for HTTP Basic auth against the WebDAV server. For B2 backend, use B2_KEY_ID instead.
storage.WEBDAV_PASS: Password for HTTP Basic auth against the WebDAV server. For B2 backend, use B2_APPLICATION_KEY instead.
storage.B2_KEY_ID: Backblaze B2 application key ID. Required when STORAGE_TYPE=b2. Together with B2_APP_KEY and B2_BUCKET, forms the minimum B2 configuration.
storage.B2_APP_KEY: Backblaze B2 application key (secret). Required when STORAGE_TYPE=b2. Treat this value as confidential; do not expose it to the client.
storage.B2_BUCKET: Backblaze B2 bucket name. Required when STORAGE_TYPE=b2. The bucket must already exist in your B2 account.
storage.B2_DOWNLOAD_URL: Optional custom download endpoint for B2 files (e.g. a Cloudflare CDN URL). When set, public file links use this URL instead of the default B2 download host.
storage.B2_S3_ENDPOINT: Optional custom S3-compatible API endpoint for B2. Overrides the default Backblaze endpoint; useful for third-party S3-compatible gateways.

smtp.SMTP_USER: Username for SMTP authentication. For Gmail, use the full email address.
smtp.SMTP_PASS: Password for SMTP authentication. For Gmail with 2FA, use an App Password.
smtp.SMTP_FROM: From address used on outgoing emails. Defaults to SMTP_USER if unset.
smtp.SMTP_SECURE: Set to true to force TLS. Auto-enabled when SMTP_PORT is 465.

cron.CRON_SECRET: Shared secret required by the Authorization: Bearer header on /api/cleanup. Unset disables the endpoint with a 401.

clerk.NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY: Clerk publishable key (frontend-visible). When set together with CLERK_SECRET_KEY, Clerk is enabled.
clerk.CLERK_SECRET_KEY: Clerk secret key (server-side only) for verifying sessions and calling the Clerk API.
clerk.CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET: Signing secret used to verify Svix webhooks from Clerk on /api/webhooks/clerk.

system.SKIP_DB_INIT: Developer-only flag. When set to true, scripts/db-init.mjs skips the init / push step.
system.DISABLE_HMR: Developer-only flag. When set to true, webpack watch is fully ignored (useful for non-React tooling).
system.NODE_ENV: Node environment, set automatically by Next.js (development / production / test).
system.VERCEL: Set to 1 by the Vercel build platform. Used to detect Vercel deployments at runtime.
system.VERCEL_URL: Deployment URL of the current Vercel build (no protocol). Used as a fallback for APP_URL.
system.VERCEL_PROJECT_PRODUCTION_URL: Production deployment URL injected by Vercel after a custom domain is attached. Preferred over VERCEL_URL.

page.title: Custom Page
page.notConfiguredDesc: Please set WEBDAV_URL, WEBDAV_USER, WEBDAV_PASS for WebDAV backends, or set STORAGE_TYPE=b2 with B2_* variables for B2 backend, in .env.local and restart, then place HTML files in pages/.
page.indexSubtitle: Driven by the storage backend's `pages/` directory (WebDAV or B2). Private pages are marked with a lock and require a password on first visit.
page.indexEmptyHint: Place `.html` files under the `pages/` directory in your configured storage backend (WebDAV or B2) and they will appear here automatically.
